stem: A researcher surveyed 200 randomly selected students at a large university and found that 68% of them prefer online classes over in-person classes. Which of the following statements is most appropriate based on this survey?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Exactly 68% of all students at the university prefer online classes.

choices.label: B
choices.text: It is reasonable to conclude that most students at the university prefer online classes.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The survey proves that online classes are better than in-person classes.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The results cannot be used to make any conclusions about the university's student population.

explanation: When a random sample shows that 68% of students prefer online classes, this provides evidence that a majority (more than 50%) of the entire student population likely prefers online classes. While we cannot say exactly 68% of all students prefer online classes due to sampling variability, the sample provides reasonable evidence for concluding that most students at the university prefer online classes. The sample was randomly selected from the population, making it appropriate to draw this general conclusion.

distractorRationale.A: This choice incorrectly assumes the sample percentage exactly equals the population percentage, ignoring sampling variability and margin of error.
distractorRationale.B: This is the correct answer as 68% provides reasonable evidence that most (>50%) students prefer online classes.
distractorRationale.C: This choice confuses preference data with quality judgment - the survey measures student preference, not which type of class is objectively better.
distractorRationale.D: This choice is too conservative - random samples from populations do allow for reasonable inferences about the population when properly conducted.
